dialogue_tree.title: Module 7: Collaborative Climate & Agenda Setting
dialogue_tree.learning_objective: Create a collaborative rather than authoritative conversational climate; Invite the colleague to set priorities collaboratively; Use transparency about time and structure; Balance flexibility with focus
dialogue_tree.description: A development discussion with a colleague about their career goals. Practice establishing a collaborative partnership and setting a shared agenda.

nodes.patient_statement: Thanks for meeting with me. I've been thinking about my career path lately. I've been in my current role for about 3 years and wondering what's next.

practitioner_choices.text: Great! I have some ideas about where you could go. Let me tell you about the options.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This jumps straight to giving advice and taking control of the agenda. You missed the opportunity to establish collaboration.

practitioner_choices.text: You're thinking about your next steps after 3 years in your current role. What's been on your mind about where you might want to go?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Perfect! This reflects their statement and invites them to set the direction. You're establishing collaboration from the start.

practitioner_choices.text: Well, you need to decide soon. Opportunities don't wait forever.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This creates pressure rather than collaboration. You're taking an authoritative stance.

nodes.patient_statement: That sounds a bit overwhelming. I'm not sure I'm ready to make big decisions yet.

practitioner_choices.text: If you don't act, you'll get stuck in your career. Is that what you want?
practitioner_choices.feedback: This is pressure and judgment disguised as a question. It damages the collaborative climate.

practitioner_choices.text: I hear that this feels like a lot. Let me step back - what would feel most useful to focus on today?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good recovery! You acknowledge the discord and return control to them.

nodes.patient_statement: I guess I'm wondering if I should look at leadership roles, or maybe specialize in a certain area, or even switch departments entirely. There are so many possibilities.

practitioner_choices.text: Lots of options indeed. We have about 30 minutes - would it help to narrow down what feels most interesting to explore?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Excellent! You're transparent about time, seek permission, and invite them to focus.

practitioner_choices.text: Let me tell you about each option so you can decide.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This takes control rather than collaborating. You're positioning yourself as the expert.

practitioner_choices.text: We need to cover all of them. You can't make an informed decision without knowing everything.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This creates pressure and doesn't honor their pace. Too many topics can overwhelm.

nodes.patient_statement: I know. But I'm not sure rushing is the right approach for me.

practitioner_choices.text: You're right that you need to move at your own pace. What would be most helpful for you in this conversation?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good pivot - you acknowledge their perspective and return control.

practitioner_choices.text: Well, you can't wait forever. Let's at least outline some options.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This continues the pressure dynamic. You're not truly collaborating.

nodes.patient_statement: I think I just want to explore what's out there. Maybe get a sense of what might fit my strengths. I'm not ready to make decisions today.

practitioner_choices.text: Exploration sounds good. You want to understand possibilities and what might align with your strengths. Should we focus on that for today?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Perfect! You affirm their approach and confirm the agenda collaboratively.

practitioner_choices.text: But we should also look at development plans and timelines.
practitioner_choices.feedback: The 'but' dismisses what they said they want. This isn't collaborative.

nodes.patient_statement: Yes, 30 minutes is good. I think leadership might be interesting to explore. I've had good feedback when I've helped train new team members.

practitioner_choices.text: So you're curious about leadership, and you've had positive experiences mentoring others. What else would you like to explore about that path?
practitioner_choices.technique: Reflection + Open question staying on focus
practitioner_choices.feedback: Excellent! You stay within the agreed focus and invite further exploration.

practitioner_choices.text: Leadership is great. Let me tell you about all the leadership opportunities available.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This shifts to expert mode rather than collaborative exploration.

nodes.patient_statement: That feels like a lot. I'm not sure I can focus on everything at once.

practitioner_choices.text: You're right, that is a lot. What feels like the most important place to start?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good recovery - validates concern and invites the colleague to prioritize.

practitioner_choices.text: We need to cover everything or you won't have the full picture.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This ignores the colleague's stated concern and maintains practitioner control.

nodes.patient_statement: I guess I'm wondering what a leadership role would actually look like day-to-day. And whether my skills really translate. Part of me thinks I could do it, but I also have doubts.

practitioner_choices.text: So there's some confidence that you could lead, and also some uncertainty about whether your skills fully translate. What would help you explore that?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Excellent! You reflect both sides and invite them to direct the exploration.

practitioner_choices.text: Actually, we should also talk about specialization options since leadership isn't the only path.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This abandons the agreed focus without checking. Not collaborative.

nodes.patient_statement: I think understanding what leadership actually involves day-to-day would help. And maybe talking to someone currently in a lead role? That would give me a better sense of whether it's right for me.
nodes.outcome: Good engagement - Through collaborative agenda-setting and staying focused on what mattered to the colleague, they identified a realistic first step.
nodes.learning_summary: Collaborative climate and focusing involve inviting the colleague to set priorities, being transparent about time and structure, and staying within agreed-upon topics. This builds partnership and increases colleague engagement in the development process.

nodes.patient_statement: I don't think this is helping. Maybe I'll figure it out on my own.
nodes.patient_context: Colleague disengages due to non-collaborative approach
nodes.outcome: Poor engagement - The non-collaborative, directive approach damaged engagement and the colleague withdrew from the conversation.
nodes.learning_summary: Directive approaches, imposing agendas, lecturing, and shifting focus without permission damage the collaborative relationship. When colleagues feel unheard or controlled, they often disengage from the conversation.
